preamble: The nasalized low vowel [ã] diphthongizes to [ou] through backing and rounding, developing a labial off-glide. This outcome is less common than [aw̃] but occurs in Romance dialects when nasalized vowels undergo diphthongization combined with denasalization. The shift represents vowel movement from low central position toward high back rounded position with glide formation.

examples.context: More common outcome: [a_nas] → [au] nasal diphthong; Latin -anum → -ão
Mid nasal vowel diphthongization; /õ/ becomes diphthong in Brazilian Portuguese dialects
Rare denasalized diphthongization with backing and rounding; limited documentation

period: Medieval-Modern (14th century onwards); Ongoing (modern dialect variation)
